frameworks.name: hierarchical Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: visual cortex implements probabilistic inference along its hierarchy

frameworks.name: particle filtering
frameworks.one_line: proposed algorithm for cortical interactive computation

frameworks.name: Bayesian belief propagation
frameworks.one_line: proposed algorithm for integrating top-down priors with bottom-up evidence

frameworks.name: feedforward static filter view
frameworks.one_line: traditional view of V1/V2 as static spatiotemporal feature extractors

core_question: How can recurrent feedforward/feedback loops across visual cortical areas be understood as a probabilistic inference machine?
key_assumptions: cortex integrates top-down contextual priors with bottom-up observations
long-latency neural responses reflect interactive rather than purely feedforward processing
visual processing is hierarchically organized across cortical areas
key_findings: early visual neurons exhibit long-latency responses indicating multi-level information processing
V1/V2 activity is tightly coupled with higher visual areas rather than purely feedforward
interpretations: recurrent cortical loops implement concurrent probabilistic inference along the visual hierarchy
particle filtering and belief propagation are plausible algorithmic models of these computations
